# GraphMyCode

> GraphMyCode visualizes any codebase as an interactive dependency graph running entirely in the browser. No uploads, no server, no account required. Uses WebAssembly (tree-sitter, Hugging Face transformers) for in-browser parsing and semantic analysis. Free and open source (PolyForm Noncommercial License).

## Product

- [GraphMyCode](https://graphmycode.com): Main application — interactive code graph visualization tool for developers

## Features

- Structural View: Interactive graph of files, classes, functions, imports, and call relationships. Answers "what does this file import?", "who calls this function?", "which modules are isolated?"
- Semantic 3D: Groups nodes by code similarity using local WASM embeddings (HuggingFace transformers + UMAP + Leiden). Finds duplicated logic and similar modules.
- Technical Debt City: 3D city metaphor where building height represents technical debt (coupling metrics). Identifies the hardest files to change.
- Dependency Heatmap: Coupling visualization with bidirectional edge detection (orange edges). Detects import cycles and spaghetti code patterns.
- AI Q&A (optional): Connect your own API key (OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic, or Ollama) to ask natural-language questions about the codebase.

## Supported Languages

JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, Rust, C, C++, C#, PHP, Ruby, Swift

## Tech Stack

React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS v4, Sigma.js, Three.js, web-tree-sitter (WASM), @huggingface/transformers (WASM), UMAP, Leiden algorithm, Graphology, ForceAtlas2

## Input Methods

- ZIP file upload (code never leaves the browser)
- Public GitHub repository URL (max 250 source files)

## Privacy

All analysis runs in-browser via WebAssembly. No code is sent to any server. No telemetry. No personal data collected. When the tab is closed, everything disappears.

## Source & License

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/franciscovaleromartin/graphmycode): Source code under PolyForm Noncommercial License 1.0.0

## Video

- [Demo on YouTube](https://youtu.be/rMAzZp0FTL4): Full product demo showing all four visualization views (Structural, Semantic 3D, Technical Debt City, Dependency Heatmap)
